title: Urban Heat Stress Mortality Risk In South Asia
summary: Build a verified workflow for identifying where urban heat island effects, wet-bulb temperature extremes, and population vulnerability create the highest heat-stress mortality risk in South Asian cities.
why_it_matters: South Asia faces some of the highest wet-bulb temperatures on Earth, approaching the 35°C survivability limit during extreme heat events. The 2015 heatwave killed over 2,500 people in India and Pakistan. Urban heat island effects add 3-8°C to background temperatures in dense cities. Mortality is concentrated among outdoor workers, slum dwellers, and elderly populations without cooling access. City-scale risk maps that combine heat exposure, population vulnerability, and cooling-access data are nearly absent.

success_metrics.target: Heat-exposure maps validated against ground-station data where available.
success_metrics.validation_method: Compare satellite-derived LST to station measurements.

success_metrics.target: Risk maps combine exposure, population density, and vulnerability indicators.
success_metrics.validation_method: Reviewer confirms vulnerability data sources and methodology.

success_metrics.target: No heat-action-plan recommendation merged without review and replication.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review, replication.
